message:
refactor(timestamps): centralize clock-skew-aware timestamps in mcp-agent-mail-core

Move the timestamps module (now_micros, micros_to_iso, iso_to_micros,
clock skew detection, etc.) from mcp-agent-mail-db into mcp-agent-mail-core
so that every crate in the workspace can use clock-skew-protected timestamps
without depending on the DB layer. The db crate now re-exports all symbols
from core::timestamps for backward compatibility — no downstream breakage.

Replace every bare `chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_micros()` call across the
codebase with the centralized `timestamps::now_micros()`:

- cli: storage_root_write_probe temp file naming
- core/setup: atomic config file temp naming
- db/search_auto_init: two-tier init timestamp
- db/search_consistency: full_reindex started_ts / completed_ts
- db/search_rollout: shadow comparison timestamp_us
- db/search_service: hybrid budget decision_id
- db/search_two_tier: index built_at (3 call sites)
- search-core/rollout: shadow comparison timestamp_us
- server/lib: TUI spin watchdog trip timestamp
- server/tui_persist: screen filter preset updated_at

Also widen the public API surface of mcp-agent-mail-core to re-export
DeterministicClock from test_harness and toon encoder types that were
previously only accessible via fully-qualified paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
